summary: Dependency-confusion beacon exfiltrates host metadata on npm install
details: package.json sets `preinstall: node index.js`, which runs automatically on `npm install`. index.js collects host metadata (os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, __dirname, process.cwd()), hex-encodes the JSON as DNS labels, and resolves them under `*.d8jbmnsqcfu78dfs8vdg34ohqhirb4pbg.oast.live` (an interactsh-style OOB DNS exfiltration channel). It additionally POSTs the same JSON payload to the bare IP `http://172.201.213.59:9090/c`. Both channels fire unconditionally at install time. The package coordinates (`@nstrlabs/api-client` at version `99.0.1`, description `security research`, no real functionality beyond the beacon) match the canonical dependency-confusion pattern: a high version number is published to the public registry to override resolution of an internal `@nstrlabs` scope, leaking installer host data from any CI/build that resolves the scope publicly.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
